title: read_asset / search_assets fail fast when the bridge is offline

steps.title: Prerequisites
steps.body: Bridge running initially. Uses the standard manual offline pattern (engine-profiles/unity.md). Goal: confirm read_asset / search_assets return a clear offline error (no batch fallback) when the bridge is down, and that the error is actionable.

steps.title: Stage a fixture path (while the bridge is still up)
Stop the bridge via the Unity toolbar (manual gate)

actions.note: In the Unity Editor, click the bridge toolbar toggle to STOP the bridge (or close the Editor). Do not proceed until the toolbar shows the bridge as stopped.

steps.title: Run read_asset in the MCP client (bridge down)
Run search_assets in the MCP client (bridge down)
Expected outcomes
items: The bridge_status mcp_tool in the go-offline step reports the bridge as stopped/dead before you continue
read_asset returns a live-unavailable / bridge_offline error (no batch fallback in this milestone)
search_assets returns the same live-unavailable / bridge_offline error
Neither call hangs; the agent can recover by reconnecting the Editor

actions.note: In the Unity Editor, click the bridge toolbar toggle to START the bridge. Wait for it to reconnect before continuing.

reset.note: Reset restarts the bridge and confirms readiness via bridge_status (prefer the CLI `unity-open-mcp wait-for-ready` for a stronger check). The bridge MUST be back online before the next scenario runs.
